title: Steffensia scabra
intro: Steffensia scabra, also known as Steffensia or Brazilian Steffensia, is a flowering plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and combat bacterial infections. The bioactive constituents of Steffensia scabra include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as gentiophylline, steffensine, and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Steffensia scabra is often used in traditional herbal medicine, and it is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, as well as being used in capsules or tinctures, to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Steffensia scabra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family includes approximately 13,000 species of plants commonly known as the coffee family or madder family. Steffensia scabra is a part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the snapdragon, gardenia, and foxglove families. The plant's classification is further divided into the subfamily Rubioideae and tribe Spermacoceae. Overall, Steffensia scabra's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships to other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
